stem: A contractor is designing a rectangular swimming pool. The length of the pool is to be 3 feet longer than the width. If the area of the pool will be 70 square feet, what will be the length, in feet, of the pool?
rationale: The correct answer is 10. Let w represent the width of the pool, in feet. Then the length is (w + 3) feet, and the area is w(w + 3) = 70. Expanding gives w2 + 3w − 70 = 0, which factors as (w − 7)(w + 10) = 0. Since w must be positive, w = 7. The length is w + 3 = 10 feet.
